Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Show HN: Stacked Game of Life 141 upvotes, 24 comments
  2. Elevator Saga: The elevator programming game (2015) 77 upvotes, 13 comments
  3. MyFirst Kids Watch Hacked. Access to Camera and Microphone 57 upvotes, 9 comments
  4. Moss is a pixel canvas where every brush is a tiny program 17 upvotes, 1 comments
  5. Qwen3.5 Fine-Tuning Guide – Unsloth Documentation 147 upvotes, 42 comments
  6. Making Firefox's right-click not suck with about:config 14 upvotes, 1 comments
  7. Libre Solar – Open Hardware for Renewable Energy 115 upvotes, 35 comments
  8. Motorola GrapheneOS devices will be bootloader unlockable/relockable 1127 upvotes, 452 comments
  9. A Visual Guide to DNA Sequencing 31 upvotes, 6 comments
  10. RE#: how we built the fastest regex engine in F# 156 upvotes, 55 comments
  11. NanoGPT Slowrun: Language Modeling with Limited Data, Infinite Compute 8 upvotes, 0 comments
  12. Notes on Project Oberon 8 upvotes, 0 comments
  13. Something is afoot in the land of Qwen 217 upvotes, 86 comments
  14. “It turns out” (2010) 157 upvotes, 61 comments
  15. Medical journal says the case reports it has published for 25 years are fiction 109 upvotes, 41 comments
  16. Sea level much higher than assumed in most coastal hazard assessments 49 upvotes, 17 comments
  17. RFC 9849. TLS Encrypted Client Hello 222 upvotes, 107 comments
  18. Better JIT for Postgres 128 upvotes, 58 comments
  19. Government grant-funded research should not be published in for-profit journals 221 upvotes, 111 comments
  20. A CPU that runs entirely on GPU 205 upvotes, 102 comments
  21. Agentic Engineering Patterns 389 upvotes, 216 comments
  22. Nobody Gets Promoted for Simplicity 602 upvotes, 358 comments
  23. Glaze by Raycast 139 upvotes, 79 comments
  24. Emails to Outlook.com rejected due to a fault or overzealous blocking rules 82 upvotes, 53 comments
  25. Raspberry Pi Pico as AM Radio Transmitter 12 upvotes, 4 comments
  26. Greg Knauss Is Losing Himself 52 upvotes, 34 comments
  27. TikTok will not introduce end-to-end encryption, saying it makes users less safe 334 upvotes, 327 comments
  28. MacBook Neo 820 upvotes, 1122 comments
  29. SRGB↔XYZ Conversion (2021) 7 upvotes, 5 comments
  30. Roboflow (YC S20) Is Hiring a Security Engineer for AI Infra 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 2:30am, 5 March 2026. Source here.
